subject: The 20 Year Master
body:
Words I like: The reason it's taking so long is because you're in a rush.

Other Words I like: It's not happening as fast as you want because you're not as good as you think you are.

Mozi Minute: The 20 Year Master

A young samurai once asked a Zen master how long it would take to achieve mastery. "10 years," the master said.

The samurai, eager to speed things up, asked "What if I work twice as hard and practice day and night?"

The master replied: "20 years."

When the confused samurai asked why it would take longer, the master explained: "If you have one eye fixed on your destination, you only have one eye left to find your way."

I see this in business every week.

The entrepreneurs most obsessed with "scaling fast" are usually the ones who scale slowest. Why?

They skip the foundations. They don't build systems. They chase shortcuts.

We had a portfolio company trying to hit $10M. Kept missing targets. Finally slowed down. Built proper systems. Fixed their operations. Revenue doubled in 6 months.

Sometimes you have to slow down to speed up.

"You can't have a baby in a month by getting 9 women pregnant" - Warren Buffett

Great feats take time. And that's why so few people accomplish them.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Want help building the right foundations to scale? We just opened spots for our next Scaling Workshop in Vegas on Feb 3/4th. Last month's attendees averaged $2.9M in revenue - we turn away 65% of applicants to keep the room quality high.

Build slow, grow fast,

Alex
